prompt.content:
---
module: yaml
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_ir, schemas_ml, schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: plain scalars stay plain and unquoted
    given: { scalars: ["Returns the string Hello, name!", "nothing to add", "it's fine", "state the exact emphasis, e.g. all caps", "yes", "colon:nospace", "on", "Off", "no"] }
    expect: { plain: true }
  - name: strings needing quoting are double-quoted
    given: { scalars: ["greet(name: string): string", "#leadinghash", " leadingspace", "trailingspace ", "true", "123", "null", "a: b", "has # hash", "- dashlead", "end colon:", "1.5", "0x10", ".inf", "~", "only ' apostrophe: with colon"] }
    expect: { double_quoted: true }
  - name: a plain-rejected string with a double quote and no apostrophe is single-quoted
    given: { scalar: "\"loudly\" is vague" }
    expect: { emitted: "'\"loudly\" is vague'" }
  - name: the empty string is double-quoted
    given: { scalar: "" }
    expect: { emitted: "\"\"" }
  - name: attribution serializes to its pinned block bytes
    given: { doc: attribution }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries:\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    file: greet.ts\n    lines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    note: builds the greeting string\n" }
  - name: IR serializes to its pinned block bytes with empty collections inline
    given: { doc: ir }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ndeps: []\ntypes: []\nfunctions:\n  - name: greet\n    signature: \"greet(name: string): string\"\n    behavior: Returns the string Hello, name!\ntests: []\n" }
  - name: an empty machine layer serializes with inline empty entries
    given: { doc: ml_empty }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries: []\n" }
  - name: a machine layer with entries serializes to its pinned block bytes
    given: { doc: ml_entries }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries:\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    kind: ambiguity\n    message: '\"loudly\" is vague'\n    reasoning: unclear whether to uppercase, add punctuation, or both\n    suggestion: state the exact emphasis, e.g. all caps\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    kind: no-op\n    message: requirement already satisfied by existing code\n    reasoning: the greeting is already emphatic enough; no edit was needed\n" }
---
# A focused YAML emitter matching `eemeli/yaml` block style

This module renders NAPL's on-disk documents (attribution, IR, machine-layer) as
YAML **byte-for-byte identical** to what the JavaScript `yaml` package
(`eemeli/yaml`) `stringify` produces for these document shapes. The exact bytes
are load-bearing ظ¤ the corpus pins them ظ¤ so reproduce the block style and scalar
heuristics precisely. It is pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain ظ¤ leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `yaml/`: `yaml/Cargo.toml` (package name `yaml`)
and `yaml/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `yaml/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes
from the workspace root before finishing.

## Builds on four modules of this workspace

Each is a sibling member crate generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external
crates.io dependencies), read their public fields to drive serialization, and do
**not** reimplement them or depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): `LineRange`, an inclusive
  1-based range with public `start`/`end` fields (`u32`).
- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): `Attribution` with public
  fields `module: String`, `target: String`, `entries: Vec<AttributionEntry>`;
  and `AttributionEntry` with public `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`,
  `lines: LineRange`, `note: String`.
- **`schemas_ir`** (`../schemas_ir`): `Ir` with public `module: String`,
  `deps: Vec<String>`, `types: Vec<IrType>`, `functions: Vec<IrFunction>`,
  `tests: Vec<IrTest>`; `IrType` with public `name`/`description`; `IrFunction`
  with public `name`/`signature`/`behavior`; `IrTest` with public `name` and
  `given`/`expect` (each a `serde_json::Value`).
- **`schemas_ml`** (`../schemas_ml`): `Ml` with public `module: String`,
  `target: String`, `entries: Vec<MlEntry>`; `MlEntry` with public
  `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `kind: MlKind`, `message: String`,
  `reasoning: String`, `suggestion: Option<String>`; and `MlKind` with variants
  `Ambiguity`, `Assumption`, `Note`, `NoOp`.

## The value model

Expose a public enum `Yaml` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`) with variants:
`Null`, `Bool(bool)`, `Int(i64)`, `Float(f64)`, `Str(String)`, `Seq(Vec<Yaml>)`,
and `Map(Vec<(String, Yaml)>)` (a mapping whose key insertion order is preserved).

## Scalar rendering

A **string scalar** renders as follows:

- The empty string renders as `""` (two double quotes).
- If the string is *plain-representable* (rules below), it renders verbatim, with
  no quoting.
- Otherwise, if it contains a double-quote character and no apostrophe, it renders
  single-quoted: wrap in `'ظخ'`, doubling any apostrophe (there are none in this
  branch). So `"loudly" is vague` ظْ `'"loudly" is vague'`.
- Otherwise it renders double-quoted: wrap in `"ظخ"`, escaping `"`ظْ`\"`, `\`ظْ`\\`,
  newlineظْ`\n`, tabظْ`\t`, carriage-returnظْ`\r`; other characters pass through.

A non-empty string is **plain-representable** only when ALL of these hold:

- Its first and last characters are not whitespace.
- It contains no newline and no control character (a control character is any
  codepoint below `0x20` other than newline, or the codepoint `0x7f`).
- Its first character is none of these indicators:
  `,` `[` `]` `{` `}` `#` `&` `*` `!` `|` `>` `'` `"` `%` `@` `` ` ``.
- If its first character is `-`, `?`, or `:`, it has a second character and that
  second character is not a space. (So `-`, `- x`, `: `, etc. are not plain, but
  `colon:nospace` is ظ¤ the leading char is a letter.)
- It does not contain `": "` (colon followed by space), does not end with `:`, and
  does not contain `" #"` (space followed by hash).
- It does not resolve as a non-string core-schema value (rule below).

A string **resolves as a non-string** when it is exactly one of the null/boolean
literals `null`, `Null`, `NULL`, `~`, `true`, `True`, `TRUE`, `false`, `False`,
`FALSE`; OR it parses as a **core integer**; OR it parses as a **core float**:

- **Core integer**: after stripping a single leading `+` or `-`: a `0x` prefix
  followed by one or more ASCII hex digits; or a `0o` prefix followed by one or
  more octal digits (`0`ظô`7`); or one or more ASCII decimal digits.
- **Core float**: after stripping a single leading `+` or `-`, the body is
  `.inf`/`.Inf`/`.INF`, or the whole string is `.nan`/`.NaN`/`.NAN`; otherwise
  split the body on `e`/`E` into a mantissa and optional exponent ظ¤ the mantissa
  must contain a `.` with all-digit (possibly empty) integer and fraction parts
  and at least one digit overall (a body with no `.` is an integer, not a float),
  and if an exponent is present it must be one or more digits after an optional
  sign.

So `yes`, `on`, `Off`, `no`, `it's fine`, `colon:nospace`, and
`Returns the string Hello, name!` stay plain; while `true`, `123`, `null`, `~`,
`1.5`, `0x10`, `.inf`, `greet(name: string): string`, `a: b`, `has # hash`,
`- dashlead`, `end colon:`, `#leadinghash`, `" leadingspace"`,
`"trailingspace "`, and `only ' apostrophe: with colon` are double-quoted.

A **non-string** scalar renders as: `Null` ظْ `null`; `Bool` ظْ `true`/`false`;
`Int` ظْ its decimal form; `Float` ظْ its Rust `f64` `to_string` form.

## Document rendering

`to_yaml_document(node: &Yaml) -> String` renders a whole document in block style
with a trailing newline (`\n`). Build the body as a list of lines joined by `\n`,
then append one `\n`.

- A **non-empty map** renders as block entries at indent 0. An **empty map**
  renders inline as `{}`. A **non-empty sequence** renders as block items at
  indent 0. An **empty sequence** renders inline as `[]`. Any other node (a
  scalar) renders as its single scalar line.

Block **map** emission at a given indent, for each `(key, value)` in order (the
key rendered as a string scalar by the rules above):

- If the value is a non-empty sequence: emit `{indent}{key}:` then the sequence as
  a block indented by `indent + 2`.
- If the value is a non-empty map: emit `{indent}{key}:` then the map as a block
  indented by `indent + 2`.
- Otherwise emit `{indent}{key}: {scalar}` on one line, where an empty sequence
  value is `[]`, an empty map value is `{}`, and any other value is its scalar
  rendering.

Block **sequence** emission at a given indent, for each item in order:

- If the item is a non-empty map: emit the map as a block at `indent + 2`, then
  turn its first line into a `- ` item ظ¤ the first emitted line is
  `{indent}- {firstLineWithoutItsLeadingIndentOfIndent+2}` and the remaining
  lines follow unchanged.
- If the item is a non-empty sequence: emit `{indent}-` then the inner sequence as
  a block at `indent + 2`.
- Otherwise emit `{indent}- {scalar}` (empty sequence ظْ `[]`, empty map ظْ `{}`,
  else the scalar rendering).

Indentation is spaces; each nesting level adds 2.

## Document serializers

`line_range(range: LineRange) -> Yaml` builds `Seq[Int(start), Int(end)]` (each
endpoint as an `Int`).

`attribution_to_yaml(attribution: &Attribution) -> String` renders the document
`Map` with keys in this order: `module` (string), `target` (string), `entries`
(a sequence; each entry is a `Map` with keys `promptLines` = `line_range(prompt_lines)`,
`file` = string, `lines` = `line_range(lines)`, `note` = string). It produces
exactly, for module `greeting`, target `typescript`, and one entry (promptLines
`[1,1]`, file `greet.ts`, lines `[1,1]`, note `builds the greeting string`):

```
module: greeting
target: typescript
entries:
  - promptLines:
      - 1
      - 1
    file: greet.ts
    lines:
      - 1
      - 1
    note: builds the greeting string
```

`ml_to_yaml(ml: &Ml) -> String` renders a `Map` with keys `module`, `target`,
`entries`. Each entry is a `Map` with keys, in order: `promptLines` =
`line_range(prompt_lines)`, `kind` = the kind string (`Ambiguity`ظْ`ambiguity`,
`Assumption`ظْ`assumption`, `Note`ظْ`note`, `NoOp`ظْ`no-op`), `message`,
`reasoning`, and ظ¤ only when `suggestion` is `Some` ظ¤ `suggestion`. An empty
entries list renders as `entries: []`.

`ir_to_yaml(ir: &Ir) -> String` renders a `Map` with keys, in order: `module`
(string), `deps` (a sequence of strings), `types` (a sequence of `Map`s with
`name`, `description`), `functions` (a sequence of `Map`s with `name`,
`signature`, `behavior`), and `tests` (a sequence of `Map`s with `name`, `given`,
`expect`, where `given`/`expect` are each a `serde_json::Value` converted to
`Yaml` by the rule below). Empty sequences render inline as `[]`.

`json_to_yaml(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Yaml` converts a JSON value: JSON
null ظْ `Null`; bool ظْ `Bool`; a number that is an exact `i64` ظْ `Int`, else
`Float` (of its `f64`); string ظْ `Str`; array ظْ `Seq` of converted items; object
ظْ `Map` of converted entries preserving key order.

attribution.note: public Yaml enum with all variants
empty string renders as two double quotes
to_yaml_document joins lines with trailing newline
root map/seq empty vs block vs scalar
emit_map iterating entries with key scalar
seq item non-empty map turned into '- ' first line
seq item inline scalar dash line
line_range builds Seq of two Ints

files.yaml.napl:
---
module: yaml
deps: [schemas_attribution, schemas_ir, schemas_ml, schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: plain scalars stay plain and unquoted
    given: { scalars: ["Returns the string Hello, name!", "nothing to add", "it's fine", "state the exact emphasis, e.g. all caps", "yes", "colon:nospace", "on", "Off", "no"] }
    expect: { plain: true }
  - name: strings needing quoting are double-quoted
    given: { scalars: ["greet(name: string): string", "#leadinghash", " leadingspace", "trailingspace ", "true", "123", "null", "a: b", "has # hash", "- dashlead", "end colon:", "1.5", "0x10", ".inf", "~", "only ' apostrophe: with colon"] }
    expect: { double_quoted: true }
  - name: a plain-rejected string with a double quote and no apostrophe is single-quoted
    given: { scalar: "\"loudly\" is vague" }
    expect: { emitted: "'\"loudly\" is vague'" }
  - name: the empty string is double-quoted
    given: { scalar: "" }
    expect: { emitted: "\"\"" }
  - name: attribution serializes to its pinned block bytes
    given: { doc: attribution }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries:\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    file: greet.ts\n    lines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    note: builds the greeting string\n" }
  - name: IR serializes to its pinned block bytes with empty collections inline
    given: { doc: ir }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ndeps: []\ntypes: []\nfunctions:\n  - name: greet\n    signature: \"greet(name: string): string\"\n    behavior: Returns the string Hello, name!\ntests: []\n" }
  - name: an empty machine layer serializes with inline empty entries
    given: { doc: ml_empty }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries: []\n" }
  - name: a machine layer with entries serializes to its pinned block bytes
    given: { doc: ml_entries }
    expect: { bytes: "module: greeting\ntarget: typescript\nentries:\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    kind: ambiguity\n    message: '\"loudly\" is vague'\n    reasoning: unclear whether to uppercase, add punctuation, or both\n    suggestion: state the exact emphasis, e.g. all caps\n  - promptLines:\n      - 1\n      - 1\n    kind: no-op\n    message: requirement already satisfied by existing code\n    reasoning: the greeting is already emphatic enough; no edit was needed\n" }
---
# A focused YAML emitter matching `eemeli/yaml` block style

This module renders NAPL's on-disk documents (attribution, IR, machine-layer) as
YAML **byte-for-byte identical** to what the JavaScript `yaml` package
(`eemeli/yaml`) `stringify` produces for these document shapes. The exact bytes
are load-bearing ظ¤ the corpus pins them ظ¤ so reproduce the block style and scalar
heuristics precisely. It is pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain ظ¤ leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `yaml/`: `yaml/Cargo.toml` (package name `yaml`)
and `yaml/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `yaml/`. Ensure `cargo test` passes
from the workspace root before finishing.

## Builds on four modules of this workspace

Each is a sibling member crate generated into this same workspace. Add a path
dependency on each in your `Cargo.toml` (workspace siblings, not external
crates.io dependencies), read their public fields to drive serialization, and do
**not** reimplement them or depend on any hand-written crate.

- **`schemas_line_range`** (`../schemas_line_range`): `LineRange`, an inclusive
  1-based range with public `start`/`end` fields (`u32`).
- **`schemas_attribution`** (`../schemas_attribution`): `Attribution` with public
  fields `module: String`, `target: String`, `entries: Vec<AttributionEntry>`;
  and `AttributionEntry` with public `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `file: String`,
  `lines: LineRange`, `note: String`.
- **`schemas_ir`** (`../schemas_ir`): `Ir` with public `module: String`,
  `deps: Vec<String>`, `types: Vec<IrType>`, `functions: Vec<IrFunction>`,
  `tests: Vec<IrTest>`; `IrType` with public `name`/`description`; `IrFunction`
  with public `name`/`signature`/`behavior`; `IrTest` with public `name` and
  `given`/`expect` (each a `serde_json::Value`).
- **`schemas_ml`** (`../schemas_ml`): `Ml` with public `module: String`,
  `target: String`, `entries: Vec<MlEntry>`; `MlEntry` with public
  `prompt_lines: LineRange`, `kind: MlKind`, `message: String`,
  `reasoning: String`, `suggestion: Option<String>`; and `MlKind` with variants
  `Ambiguity`, `Assumption`, `Note`, `NoOp`.

## The value model

Expose a public enum `Yaml` (`Debug`, `Clone`, `PartialEq`) with variants:
`Null`, `Bool(bool)`, `Int(i64)`, `Float(f64)`, `Str(String)`, `Seq(Vec<Yaml>)`,
and `Map(Vec<(String, Yaml)>)` (a mapping whose key insertion order is preserved).

## Scalar rendering

A **string scalar** renders as follows:

- The empty string renders as `""` (two double quotes).
- If the string is *plain-representable* (rules below), it renders verbatim, with
  no quoting.
- Otherwise, if it contains a double-quote character and no apostrophe, it renders
  single-quoted: wrap in `'ظخ'`, doubling any apostrophe (there are none in this
  branch). So `"loudly" is vague` ظْ `'"loudly" is vague'`.
- Otherwise it renders double-quoted: wrap in `"ظخ"`, escaping `"`ظْ`\"`, `\`ظْ`\\`,
  newlineظْ`\n`, tabظْ`\t`, carriage-returnظْ`\r`; other characters pass through.

A non-empty string is **plain-representable** only when ALL of these hold:

- Its first and last characters are not whitespace.
- It contains no newline and no control character (a control character is any
  codepoint below `0x20` other than newline, or the codepoint `0x7f`).
- Its first character is none of these indicators:
  `,` `[` `]` `{` `}` `#` `&` `*` `!` `|` `>` `'` `"` `%` `@` `` ` ``.
- If its first character is `-`, `?`, or `:`, it has a second character and that
  second character is not a space. (So `-`, `- x`, `: `, etc. are not plain, but
  `colon:nospace` is ظ¤ the leading char is a letter.)
- It does not contain `": "` (colon followed by space), does not end with `:`, and
  does not contain `" #"` (space followed by hash).
- It does not resolve as a non-string core-schema value (rule below).

A string **resolves as a non-string** when it is exactly one of the null/boolean
literals `null`, `Null`, `NULL`, `~`, `true`, `True`, `TRUE`, `false`, `False`,
`FALSE`; OR it parses as a **core integer**; OR it parses as a **core float**:

- **Core integer**: after stripping a single leading `+` or `-`: a `0x` prefix
  followed by one or more ASCII hex digits; or a `0o` prefix followed by one or
  more octal digits (`0`ظô`7`); or one or more ASCII decimal digits.
- **Core float**: after stripping a single leading `+` or `-`, the body is
  `.inf`/`.Inf`/`.INF`, or the whole string is `.nan`/`.NaN`/`.NAN`; otherwise
  split the body on `e`/`E` into a mantissa and optional exponent ظ¤ the mantissa
  must contain a `.` with all-digit (possibly empty) integer and fraction parts
  and at least one digit overall (a body with no `.` is an integer, not a float),
  and if an exponent is present it must be one or more digits after an optional
  sign.

So `yes`, `on`, `Off`, `no`, `it's fine`, `colon:nospace`, and
`Returns the string Hello, name!` stay plain; while `true`, `123`, `null`, `~`,
`1.5`, `0x10`, `.inf`, `greet(name: string): string`, `a: b`, `has # hash`,
`- dashlead`, `end colon:`, `#leadinghash`, `" leadingspace"`,
`"trailingspace "`, and `only ' apostrophe: with colon` are double-quoted.

A **non-string** scalar renders as: `Null` ظْ `null`; `Bool` ظْ `true`/`false`;
`Int` ظْ its decimal form; `Float` ظْ its Rust `f64` `to_string` form.

## Document rendering

`to_yaml_document(node: &Yaml) -> String` renders a whole document in block style
with a trailing newline (`\n`). Build the body as a list of lines joined by `\n`,
then append one `\n`.

- A **non-empty map** renders as block entries at indent 0. An **empty map**
  renders inline as `{}`. A **non-empty sequence** renders as block items at
  indent 0. An **empty sequence** renders inline as `[]`. Any other node (a
  scalar) renders as its single scalar line.

Block **map** emission at a given indent, for each `(key, value)` in order (the
key rendered as a string scalar by the rules above):

- If the value is a non-empty sequence: emit `{indent}{key}:` then the sequence as
  a block indented by `indent + 2`.
- If the value is a non-empty map: emit `{indent}{key}:` then the map as a block
  indented by `indent + 2`.
- Otherwise emit `{indent}{key}: {scalar}` on one line, where an empty sequence
  value is `[]`, an empty map value is `{}`, and any other value is its scalar
  rendering.

Block **sequence** emission at a given indent, for each item in order:

- If the item is a non-empty map: emit the map as a block at `indent + 2`, then
  turn its first line into a `- ` item ظ¤ the first emitted line is
  `{indent}- {firstLineWithoutItsLeadingIndentOfIndent+2}` and the remaining
  lines follow unchanged.
- If the item is a non-empty sequence: emit `{indent}-` then the inner sequence as
  a block at `indent + 2`.
- Otherwise emit `{indent}- {scalar}` (empty sequence ظْ `[]`, empty map ظْ `{}`,
  else the scalar rendering).

Indentation is spaces; each nesting level adds 2.

## Document serializers

`line_range(range: LineRange) -> Yaml` builds `Seq[Int(start), Int(end)]` (each
endpoint as an `Int`).

`attribution_to_yaml(attribution: &Attribution) -> String` renders the document
`Map` with keys in this order: `module` (string), `target` (string), `entries`
(a sequence; each entry is a `Map` with keys `promptLines` = `line_range(prompt_lines)`,
`file` = string, `lines` = `line_range(lines)`, `note` = string). It produces
exactly, for module `greeting`, target `typescript`, and one entry (promptLines
`[1,1]`, file `greet.ts`, lines `[1,1]`, note `builds the greeting string`):

```
module: greeting
target: typescript
entries:
  - promptLines:
      - 1
      - 1
    file: greet.ts
    lines:
      - 1
      - 1
    note: builds the greeting string
```

`ml_to_yaml(ml: &Ml) -> String` renders a `Map` with keys `module`, `target`,
`entries`. Each entry is a `Map` with keys, in order: `promptLines` =
`line_range(prompt_lines)`, `kind` = the kind string (`Ambiguity`ظْ`ambiguity`,
`Assumption`ظْ`assumption`, `Note`ظْ`note`, `NoOp`ظْ`no-op`), `message`,
`reasoning`, and ظ¤ only when `suggestion` is `Some` ظ¤ `suggestion`. An empty
entries list renders as `entries: []`.

`ir_to_yaml(ir: &Ir) -> String` renders a `Map` with keys, in order: `module`
(string), `deps` (a sequence of strings), `types` (a sequence of `Map`s with
`name`, `description`), `functions` (a sequence of `Map`s with `name`,
`signature`, `behavior`), and `tests` (a sequence of `Map`s with `name`, `given`,
`expect`, where `given`/`expect` are each a `serde_json::Value` converted to
`Yaml` by the rule below). Empty sequences render inline as `[]`.

`json_to_yaml(value: &serde_json::Value) -> Yaml` converts a JSON value: JSON
null ظْ `Null`; bool ظْ `Bool`; a number that is an exact `i64` ظْ `Int`, else
`Float` (of its `f64`); string ظْ `Str`; array ظْ `Seq` of converted items; object
ظْ `Map` of converted entries preserving key order.

entries.note: public Yaml enum with all variants
empty string renders as two double quotes
to_yaml_document joins lines with trailing newline
root map/seq empty vs block vs scalar
emit_map iterating entries with key scalar
seq item non-empty map turned into '- ' first line
seq item inline scalar dash line
line_range builds Seq of two Ints
